commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh Woe to you that plunder, and you were not plundered; and deal treacherously, and they dealt not treacherously with you. when you shall cease to plunder, you shall be plundered; and when you shall make an end of dealing treacherously, they shall deal treacherously with you. This is particularly directed to Sennacherib. (Challoner) He was a figure of persecutors of the Church, to which many passages here allude. (Calmet) Remota justitia quid sunt regna nisi magna latrocinia. Augustine, City of God iv. ) Sennacherib plundered Samaria and Juda, and despising God, was himself contemned. (Worthington).

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
